name: Grimmir, the Old Man of the Forest
description:
Grimmir, the Old Man of the Forest

*{Lore Grimmir is the Old Man of the Forest, the Alder King, the presence that watches from the shadow of the oldest oak, the cold breath on the back of the neck when the path grows dark. He is not kind, nor is he cruel -- he is wild, and the wild does not negotiate. It demands, and it takes. His followers learn to read the language of blood on snow, the geometry of root and stone, and the weight of a heart offered at the turning of the year. Grimmir does not teach harmony; he teaches respect through fear, and fear through sacrifice.

Those who walk his path become keepers of the old ways: druids who know when a grove must be fed, rangers who leave the first kill for the soil, and hedge-folk who whisper the names of spirits not to be spoken aloud. Their magic is not the gentle coaxing of garden plants but the raw, patient hunger of the forest floor -- a hunger that swallows bones and grows mushrooms in the shape of the dead.

Among the peoples of Fate's Edge, Grimmir is called by many names. In Aeler, he is the Root-Father, a patron of deep fungus and stone-lichen whose shrines are carved into living rock. In Ykrul, he is the Sky-Wolf, a spirit of the steppe who herds the seasons and grants omens. In Aelaerem, he is the Thorn-Father, keeper of hedgerow magic, honored with red thread tied around roadside stones. Wherever the wild presses against the civilized, Grimmir waits.

quote "The forest does not ask. It takes. The deer does not weep for the wolf, and the wolf does not beg the earth. Give what the season demands, or the season will take it anyway." quote

*{Domain Focus itemize Seasonal Sacrifice: the old ways of blood and offering, the turning wheel that demands payment Folk Horror: the terror of the unseen, the ritual that must be performed, the boundary that must not be crossed Primal Mystery: knowledge hidden in root and stone, the language of omens and portents Growth from Death: the forest floor as a stomach; decay as the engine of new life itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options)

A Runekeeper sworn to Grimmir does not bind a tame creature of hearth and home --- they partner with a wild spirit of the forest. Their Thiasos is a small beast or mythological creature of the wild wood: a pine marten with eyes like amber, a raven that knows the migration routes of every bird, a fox with a single white paw that leaves no prints in snow, a toad that hibernates in rotten logs and sings at the first thaw, or a stag-calf with antlers budding like spring shoots. This creature requires no food in the usual sense, but it must be allowed to roam freely in unspoiled nature each day. If confined for more than a day, it becomes Compromised (it scratches at doors, refuses to eat, and its coat grows dull; its bearer loses the benefit of Seasonal Sense until it is released).

The Codex of Grimmir is never a book of paper and ink. It may be a slab of slate carved with seasonal runes that frost over in winter, a bundle of bark scrolls sealed with pine resin, a set of knotted cords woven from the hair of a wild boar, or the hollowed horn of a stag filled with charcoal and ash. Because the wild resists being fixed in writing, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week in a natural place, re-carving or re-knotting the Rites by memory, or the symbols fade (treat as Compromised until restored under open sky).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement)

Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of Grimmir may touch a held item (weapon, tool, or garment) and whisper a word of the season. For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Lore or Stealth rolls in natural terrain, and the first time each scene the bearer would be tracked by mundane means, the tracker suffers --1 die (the forest hides their trail). After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to Grimmir.
summary: Grimmir, the Old Man of the Forest *{Lore Grimmir is the Old Man of the Forest, the Alder King, the presence that watches from the shadow of the oldest oak, the cold breath on the back of the neck when the path grows dark.

rites.name: Rite of the Blood Offering
rites.description: (Low, 4 XP) Scene; Touch; Yes. Tags: , , Materials: A drop of your own blood (1 Fatigue) or the blood of a freshly killed animal. Effect: The forest accepts your offering. For the remainder of the scene, you gain +1 die to all Survival and Stealth rolls in natural terrain. Additionally, you may ask the GM one yes/no question about a threat hidden in the wilderness (e.g., "Is there a predator watching us?"). Push It: The offering is accepted with hunger -- you gain +2 dice instead, but you must leave a visible token (a hair, a scrap of clothing) on the ground. That token can be used by the GM later as a sympathetic link (spend 1 SB to have an animal or spirit track you). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: The forest accepts your offering. For the remainder of the scene, you gain +1 die to all Survival and Stealth rolls in natural terrain. Additionally, you may ask the GM one yes/no question about a threat hidden in the wilderness (e.g., ``Is there a predator watching us?''). \\

rites.name: Rite of the Speaking Roots
rites.description: (Low, 5 XP) Scene; Zone; No. Tags: , Materials: Soil from a grave or a crossroads, held in the mouth. Effect: You listen to the memory of the land. Gain +2 dice to Investigation or Lore when seeking to understand what happened in a natural place -- a death, a ritual, a violation of the wild. The GM must give you a truthful but cryptic answer. Push It: The roots speak too clearly. You see a vision of the event, but it leaves you shaken: mark Fatigue 1 and the GM gains 1 SB. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: You listen to the memory of the land. Gain +2 dice to Investigation or Lore when seeking to understand what happened in a natural place -- a death, a ritual, a violation of the wild. The GM must give you a truthful but cryptic answer. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Twisted Path
rites.description: (Standard, 7 XP) Scene; Near; Yes. Tags: , , Materials: A knotted cord woven from thorns and hair. Effect: The forest bends against your enemies. Choose a single target within Near. For the remainder of the scene, all their navigation, tracking, and movement rolls suffer --1 die as roots trip them, branches block their way, and the path turns subtly wrong. Push It: The curse also causes the target to suffer 1 Fatigue as they struggle against the hostile woods. Mark 1 SB (Diamonds) as the forest's malice becomes visible. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: The forest bends against your enemies. Choose a single target within Near. For the remainder of the scene, all their navigation, tracking, and movement rolls suffer --1 die as roots trip them, branches block their way, and the path turns subtly wrong. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Heart's Meal
rites.description: (Standard, 9 XP) Instant; Touch; No. Tags: , , , Materials: The fresh heart of an animal (or a sentient creature, if you dare), still warm. Effect: You consume the heart raw. Gain one of the following: itemize Heal 1 Harm (the heart's vitality transfers to you). Gain a +2 die bonus on your next physical action (the heart's strength). Learn a secret the creature knew in life (GM discretion; the heart whispers as you eat). itemize After the rite, you are marked -- animals within Near range are nervous around you (disadvantage on animal handling) for one scene. Push It: Eat the heart of a thinking creature. The benefit is doubled, but you gain a Hunger Condition (must eat another heart within a month or suffer 1 Fatigue per week). The GM gains 2 SB. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Instant.
rites.effect: You consume the heart raw. Gain one of the following: itemize Heal 1 Harm (the heart's vitality transfers to you). Gain a +2 die bonus on your next physical action (the heart's strength). Learn a secret the creature knew in life (GM discretion; the heart whispers as you eat). itemize After the rite, you are marked -- animals within Near range are nervous around you (disadvantage on animal handling) for one scene. \\

rites.name: Rite of the World's Wound
rites.description: (High, 13 XP) Extended; Zone; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: A sacred grove-site, a circle of stones, an offering of blood or seed. Effect: Heal a blighted place or quicken a damaged ecosystem. Clear taint, restore fertility, awaken dormant forces. Gain +2 dice to related Lore/Nature actions in the region. Start an Ecosystem Restoration [8] timer. Push It: The cure spreads to adjacent areas but unbalances another; mark 2 SB (Diamonds). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Timer: 8 segments.
rites.effect: Heal a blighted place or quicken a damaged ecosystem. Clear taint, restore fertility, awaken dormant forces. Gain +2 dice to related Lore/Nature actions in the region. Start an Ecosystem Restoration [8] timer. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Cycle's Crown
rites.description: (High, 14 XP) Scene; Self/Zone; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: A circlet of woven branches, antlers, and seasonal tokens. Effect: Become an avatar of the cycle. Choose two: itemize Seasonal Mastery: +2 dice to all actions keyed to the current season (spring: healing and growth; summer: strength and harvest; autumn: preservation and preparation; winter: endurance and hiding). Verdant Command: Command plant life in Near automatically. Cycle's Healing: Remove one Condition or downgrade Harm by one level. Growth's Protection: Allies gain +1 Armor from natural cover. itemize Begin a Seasonal Avatar [6] timer. Push It: Extend the blessings to all present; mark +2 Obligation and gain Seasonal Attunement (see Corruption). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Timer: 6 segments.
rites.effect: Become an avatar of the cycle. Choose two: itemize Seasonal Mastery: +2 dice to all actions keyed to the current season (spring: healing and growth; summer: strength and harvest; autumn: preservation and preparation; winter: endurance and hiding). Verdant Command: Command plant life in Near automatically. Cycle's Healing: Remove one Condition or downgrade Harm by one level. Growth's Protection: Allies gain +1 Armor from natural cover. itemize Begin a Seasonal Avatar [6] timer. \\
